title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates setting up Odysseus (a workspace agent framework) and OpenCode (a build agent) for local execution using Gemma 4 via Ollama, with Venice API as a fallback provider. The tutorial covers installation in Docker, model management, agent skills and memory systems, and a hybrid approach of building with frontier models then executing tasks locally.
writeup: This tutorial walks through running AI agents locally by pairing Odysseus, a workspace agent framework, with OpenCode, a build agent, using Gemma 4 served through Ollama and the Venice API configured as a fallback provider. It covers Docker installation, model management, and the agent skills and memory systems that the two frameworks rely on. The central method is a hybrid workflow: build with frontier models, then run the resulting tasks locally. The takeaway is a concrete local setup that keeps execution on-machine while leaning on stronger remote models only for the building phase.
